lanes.hypothesis: For couples who just finished true-crime content, a hook that reframes passive watching into active solving will outperform a more cinematic hook because it directly connects to the immediate boredom trigger while showing the case quality early to reduce skepticism.

scripts.hypothesis: For couples who just finished true-crime content, a hook that reframes passive watching into active solving will outperform a more cinematic hook because it directly connects to the immediate boredom trigger while showing the case quality early to reduce skepticism.

hook.text: Finished the episode? Solve one instead.
hook.visual: A TV true-crime title screen cuts to two people dropping a remote and opening a digital case file on a laptop; quick flash of Dr. Mara Ellison's file and the Stairwell 4B scene.

body.text: Voiceover: A biotech miracle appears to cure a chronic disease, then lead scientist Dr. Mara Ellison dies in a staged accident in Cambridge. You get 4 envelopes, 21 case documents in this file, and 2-4 hours to figure out who is lying. It is instant access, so date night starts in minutes.
body.tension: The audience sees the victim, the sterile stairwell, and suspect cards for Evan Kline, Nadia Reyes, Tom Briggs, and Raina Patel without revealing the culprit.
body.value_prop: A premium, story-led mystery for two that replaces passive streaming with a same-night shared investigation.

cta.visual: On-screen CTA over dossier spread: '$19.99 - Instant access after purchase - 1-6 players' with button-style text 'Open the file tonight'.

scripts.text_overlay: Stop watching mysteries. Solve one together tonight. 4 envelopes. 21 documents. 2-4 hours.
scripts.audio_notes: Low, suspenseful pulse with soft keyboard clicks; creator-style voiceover, intimate and immediate; subtle paper shuffle SFX when documents appear.
scripts.proof_element: Real case-file preview with 21 documents, victim card, suspect board, and Stairwell 4B scene imagery from the case.
scripts.testing_variable: Hook type: direct Netflix-replacement phrasing vs cinematic case-logline hook

scripts.hypothesis: For couples who just finished true-crime content, a hook that reframes passive watching into active solving will outperform a more cinematic hook because it directly connects to the immediate boredom trigger while showing the case quality early to reduce skepticism.

hook.text: A miracle drug worked too well. Then the scientist died.
hook.visual: Fast cold open: biotech building exterior in Kendall Square, victim name card for Dr. Mara Ellison, then a flash of scene tape in the sterile stairwell.

body.text: Voiceover: This week's case starts inside a badge-locked biotech lab in Cambridge. Dr. Mara Ellison is dead, the company pulled the drug, and every suspect has a reason to protect the truth. You and your partner get instant access to 4 envelopes and 21 documents to work the case tonight.
body.tension: The cinematic setup feels like a thriller trailer, then pivots into the invitation to solve it yourselves.
body.value_prop: An adult-feeling date night with a strong plot, premium dossier visuals, and same-night access.

cta.visual: Laptop screen with open dossier and overlaid CTA: '$19.99 per case - Start in minutes'.

scripts.text_overlay: Date night with a plot. Instant access. 21 documents. Solve together.
scripts.audio_notes: Cinematic trailer-style hit on the first line, then restrained suspense bed; calm voiceover with true-crime trailer pacing.
scripts.proof_element: Case-specific story proof: Cambridge biotech setting, named victim, four suspects, and visible document previews.
scripts.testing_variable: Hook type: direct Netflix-replacement phrasing vs cinematic case-logline hook

lanes.hypothesis: For hosts needing a same-day group activity, showing exactly how the case works for a small group will convert better than pure hype because this segment worries about confusion and wants proof that setup is easy and social.

scripts.hypothesis: For hosts needing a same-day group activity, showing exactly how the case works for a small group will convert better than pure hype because this segment worries about confusion and wants proof that setup is easy and social.

hook.text: Need tonight's group activity? Use one case.
hook.visual: Phone camera style clip of friends arriving with snacks; host opens laptop and says 'We are doing this tonight' while dossier thumbnails fill the screen.

body.text: Voiceover: No costumes, no game master, no long rules talk. Just download the case, open envelope one, and let everyone crowd around the evidence. In The Miracle Withdrawal, your group works through 21 documents, a suspect board, and a biotech death staged as an accident in Cambridge.
body.tension: The reel shows document passing, friends pointing at clues, and quick flashes of the suspect cards without revealing the answer.
body.value_prop: A same-night mystery centerpiece for 3-6 people that feels more immersive than another board game and easier to run than a party mystery.

cta.visual: Friends gathered around printed and on-screen clues; overlay reads '1-6 players - 2-4 hours - Instant access'.

scripts.text_overlay: No host script. No waiting. One case for tonight.
scripts.audio_notes: Percussive, modern suspense groove; natural room laughter under VO; quick whoosh transitions between clues.
scripts.proof_element: Demo-style walkthrough showing envelope progression, suspect cards, and group play around the documents.
scripts.testing_variable: Proof style: group-play demo vs premium dossier close-up montage

scripts.hypothesis: For hosts needing a same-day group activity, showing exactly how the case works for a small group will convert better than pure hype because this segment worries about confusion and wants proof that setup is easy and social.

hook.text: This is not another cheap printable.
hook.visual: Macro close-ups of polished dossier pages, suspect portraits, timeline strip, and the annotated Stairwell 4B layout sliding across a table.

body.text: Voiceover: If you are hosting tonight, this case gives your group a real investigation to rally around. The Miracle Withdrawal includes 4 envelopes, 21 documents, and a clean clue flow built for 1-6 players. It starts with a dead scientist, a miracle drug, and four people with motive.
body.tension: Close-up visuals sell quality first, then the voiceover reveals the social use case and the mystery stakes.
body.value_prop: A premium-feeling digital mystery that avoids the low-effort PDF vibe and still works smoothly for group play tonight.

cta.text: Bring one new thing to game night.
cta.visual: Hero frame with dossier spread and overlay '$19.99 per case - Instant access after purchase'.

scripts.text_overlay: Premium dossier feel. 4 envelopes. 21 documents. Start tonight.
scripts.audio_notes: Sharper, design-forward suspense bed with camera shutter and paper-slide SFX; measured VO focused on clarity.
scripts.proof_element: Polished document close-ups including timeline strip, suspect cards, and case layout from the real case assets.
scripts.testing_variable: Proof style: group-play demo vs premium dossier close-up montage

lanes.hypothesis: For last-minute gift buyers, urgency-led messaging will perform best when paired with proof that the gift still feels thoughtful and polished, and CTA framing will determine whether they respond more to instant convenience or emotional thoughtfulness.

scripts.hypothesis: For last-minute gift buyers, urgency-led messaging will perform best when paired with proof that the gift still feels thoughtful and polished, and CTA framing will determine whether they respond more to instant convenience or emotional thoughtfulness.

hook.text: Forgot the gift? This still feels intentional.
hook.visual: A text thread saying 'Dinner tonight at 7?' cuts to a clean gift-style screen recording of the case landing page and dossier previews.

body.text: Voiceover: Instead of sending another gift card, send a mystery they can actually open tonight. Declassified Cases gives them instant access to a full 2-4 hour investigation with 4 envelopes and 1-6 player flexibility. This week's case starts with Dr. Mara Ellison's suspicious death inside a Cambridge biotech lab.
body.tension: The reel balances urgency with premium presentation, showing that the digital format still feels substantial and memorable.
body.value_prop: A last-minute digital gift that feels more personal than clutter and more memorable than a generic card.

cta.visual: Gift-forward layout with case cover, dossier preview, and on-screen text '$19.99 - Instant access after purchase'.

scripts.audio_notes: Soft suspense piano with light ticking; polished female voiceover, warm but efficient; subtle notification pop at open.
scripts.proof_element: Gift-style preview of the real dossier pages plus clear facts: 4 envelopes, 2-4 hours, 1-6 players, $19.99.
scripts.testing_variable: CTA style: convenience-led CTA vs thoughtfulness-led CTA

scripts.hypothesis: For last-minute gift buyers, urgency-led messaging will perform best when paired with proof that the gift still feels thoughtful and polished, and CTA framing will determine whether they respond more to instant convenience or emotional thoughtfulness.

hook.text: A gift they will talk about tomorrow.
hook.visual: Elegant over-shoulder shot of someone sending a digital mystery gift, then quick cuts of a couple opening the file and reacting to the suspect board.

body.text: Voiceover: It is thoughtful without needing shipping, wrapping, or guesswork. They get a premium digital case they can play solo, as a couple, or with friends. The Miracle Withdrawal drops them into a biotech conspiracy in Cambridge with 21 documents to untangle tonight.
body.tension: The story preview and recipient reactions make the gift feel vivid and usable, not abstract.
body.value_prop: An affordable experience gift with immediate use and broad fit across different play styles.

cta.visual: Final screen with gift ribbon graphic over dossier spread and CTA text 'Send a mystery gift tonight - $19.99'.

scripts.text_overlay: Gift an experience, not more stuff.
scripts.audio_notes: Refined cinematic bed with soft riser; reassuring VO, slightly more emotive than variant A.
scripts.proof_element: Recipient-use proof showing it works as solo, couple, or group play, plus visible case-file previews.
scripts.testing_variable: CTA style: convenience-led CTA vs thoughtfulness-led CTA

optimization_applied: OPT-W14-002: used digital unboxing and file-preview creative language to show exactly what buyers receive
Applied W14 learning to front-load case-file previews, document count, play time, price, and instant access in-script to reduce post-click skepticism
